title: Conjecture 1.8
statement_text: For all integers $\kappa, \ell \geq 0$, there exists $c \geq 0$ such that every graph with chromatic number greater than $c$ contains either a complete subgraph on $\kappa$ vertices or holes of $\ell$ consecutive lengths.
context_text: The authors proved in [14] (a prior paper in this series) that every triangle-free graph with sufficiently large chromatic number contains holes of $\ell$ consecutive lengths (result 1.7 of the present paper). Conjecture 1.8 extends this to graphs with clique number bounded by any $\kappa$. The conjecture remains open; this paper makes a partial step by proving 1.9, which guarantees holes of two consecutive lengths both greater than $\ell$.
notes: Explicitly labelled 'Conjecture' in the paper. The authors state it was conjectured in their earlier paper [14]; the theorem header carries no parenthetical attribution, so classified as 'states' per schema rules. Theorem 1.9 of this paper (holes of two consecutive long lengths) is proved as a step towards this conjecture.
paper_title: Induced subgraphs of graphs with large chromatic number. X. Holes of specific residue
